2606.26383.title: SOLAR: AI-Powered Speed-of-Light Performance Analysis
2606.26383.paper_summary: How fast could a deep-learning model run on target hardware, and how far is today's implementation from that limit? These questions are central to software, hardware, and algorithm optimizations. Speed-of-Light (SOL) analysis answers them by computing a workload's theoretical minimum execution time on a given architecture. Yet deriving SOL bounds remains manual, error-prone, and disconnected from rapid model development. To close this gap, we introduce SOLAR, a framework that automatically derives validated SOL bounds from PyTorch and JAX source code. SOLAR leverages both generative and deterministic components in its flow: an LLM frontend translates any source programs into an executable Affine Loop IR, validated by output comparison; a deterministic flow lifts the IR into an einsum graph; and an analytical backend computes unfused, fused, and cache-aware SOL bounds. SOLAR provides comprehensive operator and language coverage, produces validated bounds with zero observed SOL violations, and offers multi-fidelity analysis that tightens bounds and surfaces optimization insights. We evaluate SOLAR across KernelBench, JAX/Flax models, and robotics workloads. These experiments demonstrate four use cases: headroom analysis at multiple fidelity levels, identifying optimization opportunities, cross-platform exploration, and inverse-roofline hardware provisioning.
2606.26383.paper_summary_zh: 如何評估深度學習模型在目標硬體上的運行速度，以及當前實現與該極限之間的距離？這些問題對於軟體、硬體和演算法優化至關重要。光速（SOL）分析通過計算在給定架構上工作負載的理論最小執行時間來回答這些問題。然而，推導SOL邊界仍然是手動的、易出錯的，並且與快速模型開發脫節。為了縮小這一差距，我們引入了SOLAR，一個能夠自動從PyTorch和JAX源代碼推導經過驗證的SOL邊界的框架。SOLAR在其流程中利用了生成和確定性組件：一個LLM前端將任何源程序轉換為可執行的Affine Loop IR，通過輸出比較進行驗證；一個確定性流程將IR提升為einsum圖；而一個分析後端計算未融合、融合和考慮緩存的SOL邊界。SOLAR提供全面的運算子和語言覆蓋，產生經過驗證的邊界，並且觀察到的SOL違規為零，還提供多忠實度分析，進一步收緊邊界並揭示優化見解。我們在KernelBench、JAX/Flax模型和機器人工作負載上評估了SOLAR。這些實驗展示了四個用例：在多個忠實度級別進行的潛力分析、識別優化機會、跨平台探索和逆屋頂線硬體配置。
2606.26383.author: Qijing Huang et.al.
2606.26383.authors: Qijing Huang, Sana Damani, Zhifan Ye, Athinagoras Skiadopoulos, Siva Kumar Sastry Hari, Jason Clemons, Sahil Modi, Jingquan Wang, Aditya Kane, Edward C Lin, Humphrey Shi, Christos Kozyrakis
